abstract: Creates a layer object that is associated with a graphics context.

declaration.code: init?(_ context: CGContext, size: CGSize, auxiliaryInfo: CFDictionary?)

overview:
## Return Value

A CGLayer object. In Objective-C, you’re responsible for releasing this object using the function [doc://com.apple.coregraphics/documentation/CoreGraphics/CGLayerRelease] when you no longer need the layer.

## Discussion

After you create a [doc://com.apple.coregraphics/documentation/CoreGraphics/CGLayer] object, you should reuse it whenever you can to facilitate the Core Graphics caching strategy. Core Graphics caches any objects that are reused, including [doc://com.apple.coregraphics/documentation/CoreGraphics/CGLayer] objects. Objects that are reused frequently remain in the cache. In contrast, objects that are used once in a while may be moved in and out of the cache according to their frequency of use. If you don’t reuse [doc://com.apple.coregraphics/documentation/CoreGraphics/CGLayer] objects, Core Graphics won’t cache them. This means that you lose an opportunity to improve the performance of your application.

# init(_:size:auxiliaryInfo:)

**Initializer**

Creates a layer object that is associated with a graphics context.

## Declaration

```swift
init?(_ context: CGContext, size: CGSize, auxiliaryInfo: CFDictionary?)
```

## Parameters

- **context**: The graphics context you want to create the layer relative to. The layer uses this graphics context as a reference for initialization.
- **size**: The size, in default user space units, of the layer relative to the graphics context.
- **auxiliaryInfo**: Reserved for future use. Pass `NULL`.

## Return Value

A CGLayer object. In Objective-C, you’re responsible for releasing this object using the function [doc://com.apple.coregraphics/documentation/CoreGraphics/CGLayerRelease] when you no longer need the layer.

## Discussion

After you create a [doc://com.apple.coregraphics/documentation/CoreGraphics/CGLayer] object, you should reuse it whenever you can to facilitate the Core Graphics caching strategy. Core Graphics caches any objects that are reused, including [doc://com.apple.coregraphics/documentation/CoreGraphics/CGLayer] objects. Objects that are reused frequently remain in the cache. In contrast, objects that are used once in a while may be moved in and out of the cache according to their frequency of use. If you don’t reuse [doc://com.apple.coregraphics/documentation/CoreGraphics/CGLayer] objects, Core Graphics won’t cache them. This means that you lose an opportunity to improve the performance of your application.
title: init(_:size:auxiliaryInfo:)
